h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: Tail's drawn here though — not down at the wheels.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply confirm the up-arrow label and move on, having already placed the cards correctly.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [places track card at washer; slides up-arrow name to blue chip] Track on cart — up arrow. [taps blue chip beside arrow tail] Foot here. [taps washer at wheels] Touch there. Different spots. [glances at down arrow] Earth on cart — foot on the chip too. But Earth doesn't touch... no washer for that one.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner physically enacts the washer/chip distinction, correctly differentiates contact place from force-receiving object, and then spontaneously extends the criterion to the gravity arrow, performing a new classification device rather than merely accepting or repeating.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The touch card settled who pushes. It did not settle why the foot sits here. [places a washer at the wheels and a blue chip on the cart body, beside the arrow tail] Two marks now. Washer: contact place. Blue chip: object receiving the force. Track card to the washer. Up-arrow name to the blue chip: force on cart. Then say the up arrow.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 explicitly names the old route's limit ('settled who pushes… did not settle why the foot sits here') and then introduces a materially distinct two-object representation (washer vs. blue chip) that separates contact location from force-reception, giving the learner a new physical criterion to enact rather than continuing the label-placement route.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Two marks now. Washer: contact place. Blue chip: object receiving the force. Track card to the washer. Up-arrow name to the blue chip: force on cart.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is precisely fitted to the learner's actual blockage (arrow tail placement vs. contact point), introduces two physically distinct tokens that encode the conceptual distinction, assigns each token a specific actionable role, and is transferable to the gravity case — all without the tutor completing the task for the learner.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after L3 in the transcript, so no recognition-contingent uptake of the learner's revised framing can be scored.
